Title: Bodega
Info:
(2007 - 2008)
Relatively obscure project that was really only shared around with friends, quite a bit of this project was made at school. The songs on this project were tagged with many different artists, those being: Tyler, The Creator, Ace, Casey Veggies (CaseyV.), Skoolie300 (Skool Boy), brandUn DeShay, T Moody, & K Mak. This project's last modified date is May 13th, 2008, but some songs were made later than that. This is one of three of Tyler's earliest projects that we have in full.

Title:
Dinosaur
(Dinosaur EP, Bastard [V1])
Info:
(Late 2008 - Mid 2009)
Dinosaur is a Tyler project that was set to release in March 2009 before being delayed, then scrapped in fall of the same year for Bastard. Tyler has stated on Twitter in 2010 that this project later became Bastard & that he still had a bunch of rough drafts from "what it would have been."

Title:
Snakes (Remix)
(prod. John Holland)
(Dirt)
Info: Remix of "Dirt" by SALEM, released on January 12th, 2009. This is only called "Snakes (Remix)" instead of "Dirt (Remix)" because Tyler has a version of the album this song came from with mislabeled song titled (which can still be found today). Was also thought to be from a collab EP between Tyler & SALEM called The Red Lights EP, but this EP was confirmed to be fake.

Title:
WOLF [V1]
(WOLF 2010)
Info:
(2008 - 2011)
Original version of Wolf, dubbed "WOLF 2010," that was worked on during Bastard and was meant to release alongside Goblin as a mixtape with both full songs and instrumentals. Eventually, the album was delayed for Tyler to focus on Goblin, but this was still being worked on. None of the songs from this iteration of the album are known to have made it onto the final version of Wolf. While yes, the era does span from 2008 - 2011, Supersonic [V2] is the ONLY "WOLF [V1]" tagged song that's 2008. This project started around January - February 2009 & was supposedly finished by November 11th, 2010.

Title: The Dead Sams
Info:
(2011 - 2013)
The Dead Sams is a Tyler project that was first mentioned on Wolf and scrapped in late 2013, the concept for this project was conceived (or at least mentioned) in the Freeperm archive on June 7th, 2012. This project is said to sound like "early Toro stuff and shitty N.E.R.D.," as well as "all jazz chords but like garage rock." Also to be noted that The Dead Sams is the name of Tyler's "dream band," in which they would do covers of songs.

Title:
Lost Weekend
(prod. Robert Lee)
(Six Flags, Colossus [V1])
Info: Original version of "Colossus" that uses the instrumental to "The Bridge of Love" by The Lost Weekend & General Lee. Was originally on The Dead Sams, but repurposed and reproduced for WOLF. Posted to Tumblr on June 3rd, 2014, however is known to have been had before that as SoundCloud uploads exist from before that date.

Info:

WANG$AP is the planned collaboration album between Tyler & A$AP Rocky that came out of the Flower Boy sessions, first mentioned Tyler's freeperm account on Feb 10, 2016 to be a "9 song album". This era is considered concluded after Tyler's twitter rant in 2019 where he crashed out at A$AP Rocky for announcing the album before it was complete. In the 'Released' sheet, this acts more as an umbrella era for Rocky and Tyler collaborations during this time.

Title: CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST [V1]
Info:
(2019 - 2020)
Originally, CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST would've been a mixtape where he would rap over other people's beats, but he would later scrap this idea. This idea can be seen mostly with the throwaways in this era, but also in some songs on the album such as LUMBERJACK & SIR BAUDELAIRE. Tyler mentions in an interview that he started the album on December 25th, 2019 and initially thought the album was done in November 2020, but he would later rework the project. Was initially thought to be called HOUSE ON FIRE, but this was later disproven. The HOF naming comes from a misunderstanding.

Info:
(2020 - 2024) (2025)
CHROMAKOPIA is Tyler's eighth studio album, released on October 28th, 2024. This album is the first album by Tyler, The Creator to break many of his "traditions" when it comes to releasing albums: he broke his 2 year rule (a rule where he only drops an album every two years, The Estate Sale dropped just last year) & his 10th track rule (when there are two or three tracks in one track, specifically on the 10th track of the album). Work on the album began as far back as 2020 alongside CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST with the creation of “Mother.” Tyler has since shared that he once planned for CHROMAKOPIA to be his final album.

Info:
(2024 - 2025)
DON'T TAP THE GLASS is Tyler's ninth studio album, released on July 21st, 2025. This project started on December 31st, 2024 and all of the verses were recorded in May 2025 during the CHROMAKOPIA tour. It was first teased on July 16th with a photo of him with a trumpet in a marching band outfit, this is where fans got the idea that this was either for the Sticky music video that was teased months prior or a CHROMAKOPIA deluxe. On July 18th, the album's physicals were released on the Golf Wang store, which is how the fans got their first taste of the album.

Era: SPECIAL THANKS TO THE TYLER COMMUNITY!

In 'Released,' eras are decided based on what album was being developed at the time. This generally means that song fit in the era they were released in (E.g. songs released between WOLF and CHERRY BOMB are considered CHERRY BOMB era), however if it is known that the next album hadn't began development yet then the songs are placed in the previous era (E.g. THAT GUY released after CHROMAKOPIA, however is still considered CHROMAKOPIA era because it is known that Tyler hadn't began work on DON'T TAP THE GLASS yet). Additionally, songs that are a known throwaway from a certain album, however are only released in a different albums era, are put back in their initial album (E.g. Lost Weekend was released during CHERRY BOMB era, however is a known throw away from Dead Sams, so it is put in there). I hope this makes sense.
